rulings.Case Title: ADOLOFO URTECHO et al VS. LUIS SEGURA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Why Order For Sale Of Dwelling Should Not Issue - 1969 San Jose Ave
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, July 10, 2015 line 1. Order To Show Cause Why Order For Sale Of Dwelling Should Not Issue - 1969 San Jose Ave Continued to August 17, 2015. Judgment creditor is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6B and provide courtesy copies of Application and all supporting documents no later than July 24, 2015 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. Judgment creditor shall also provide notice of the new hearing date to the judgment debtors and occupants of the property at issue.= (501/JKS)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN WOODRUFF ET AL VS. MICHAEL VERLATTI
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, July 10, 2015 line 2. DEFENDANT MICHAEL VERLATTI REVOCABLE TRUST's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT sustained with 10 day leave to amend. = (501/JKS)

rulings.Case Title: 345 SIXTH STREET LLC VS. WOODBANK INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And For Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, July 10, 2015 line 3. PLAINTIFF 345 SIXTH STREET LLC's Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And For Documents And For Monetary Sanctions DENIED. Defendant/Cross-Complainant is a suspended corporation. (Pascal Declaration 5). A suspended corporation is disabled from participating in any litigation activities, including discovery. See Palm Valley Homeowners Ass's v. Design MTC (2000) 85 Cal.App.4th 553. = (501/JKS)

rulings.Case Title: MIKHAIL VAYNBERG VS. KRISTIE MILLER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment Pursuant To Breah Of Stipulation And Possession Of The Premises
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, July 10, 2015 line 4. PLAINTIFF MIKHAIL VAYNBERG's Motion For Judgment Pursuant To Breach Of Stipulation And Possession Of The Premises GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is GRANTED as to the request for judgment for possession, rent ($1,900) and daily damages ($4,180.44). Motion is DENIED as to the request for attorney's fees and costs pursuant to the attorney fees clause in the Lease Agreement. No Lease Agreement provided and no evidence provided to determine reasonableness of the fees requested. (Complaint 17(d)). = (501/JKS)
